observations.summary: A character accuses another of their temple's fall due to a blinded man and questions loyalty, suggesting a higher deity El deserves service instead.

evidence.quote: "Your temple fell because of a blinded man," he snarled, the hesitation hardening. "You crumpled before the sacred chest. Why should I serve you rather than El, who dwells higher?"

observations.notes: This reflects a pivotal moment of challenge and doubt towards a deity figure, highlighting themes of faith, hierarchy, and failure.
